results.title: UKG Accelerates Global Growth with Strategic Acquisition of UK-based Mo | UKG
results.text:
# UKG Accelerates Global Growth with Strategic Acquisition of UK-based Mo

LONDON, UK - 2025-11-26

## Builds on previous acquisitions of UK&I companies Quorbit and Immedis, enabling UKG to deliver next-generation workforce solutions from the front office to the frontline 

UKG, a leading global AI platform that unifies HR, pay, and workforce management, has strengthened its position as the world’s Workforce Operating Platform for frontline-focused organisations through the strategic acquisition of UK-based technology innovator Mo.

Founded in London in 2013, Mo developed an award-winning culture-building platform that makes often unseen work visible, helping organisations celebrate achievements, recognise frontline worker contributions, and improve team morale. Its customers include brands such as Crowne Plaza Hotels and Resorts, Ocado Retail, and OVO Energy. With this acquisition, Mo's innovative employee rewards and recognition technology is being integrated into UKG Beacon, an intelligent and data-driven employee engagement platform designed specifically for small- to mid-sized businesses.

“Joining UKG allows us to bring the power of recognition to more organisations and more people than ever before,” said Luke Fisher, former Chief Executive Officer at Mo, who will stay on at UKG in a senior product management role. “Together, we’re making it easier for every business to build cultures where appreciation is part of everyday work, especially for critical frontline workers. By combining Mo’s innovation with UKG’s scale and unmatched workforce data, we’re accelerating our shared mission to help organisations create stronger, more connected, and more human workplaces.”

The acquisition follows Mo’s participation in UKG Labs, an accelerator program that connects UKG customers with early-stage start-ups and supports UKG's vision for human-centred innovations that amplify the moments that matter most, helping to create great workplace cultures.

This latest UK&I acquisition expands UKG’s ability to deliver industry-leading solutions that address the unique needs of frontline worker-heavy organisations:

- In 2023, the company acquired Immedis, a global payroll provider based in Kilkenny, Ireland, which accelerated and enhanced the development of UKG One View, an AI-powered multi-country payroll experience supporting 20+ languages across 160 countries. One View ensures globally compliant, accurate pay slips, transforming the traditionally fragmented and costly global payroll model.
- In 2022, UKG acquired Oxford-based startup, Quorbit, which specialised in long-term workforce planning for frontline and hourly workers. Quorbit’s technology is now part of UKG Strategic Workforce Planning, a platform that enables organisations to forecast staffing needs years ahead and align operational planning with employee hiring and development.

“Our successful track record of UK and Ireland -based acquisitions has enabled UKG to

results.title: UKG (Ultimate Kronos Group) Review 2026 – Features, Pros, Cons & Pricing
results.author: Scott Charles
results.text:
UKG (Ultimate Kronos Group) Review 2026 – Features, Pros, Cons & Pricing 

 

## UKG (Ultimate Kronos Group) Overview & 2026 Industry Position

UKG (Ultimate Kronos Group) is a leading provider of HCM (human capital management) solutions, formed from the 2020 merger between Ultimate Software and Kronos Incorporated. Combining decades of workforce management expertise with innovative cloud-based HR technologies, UKG serves over 70,000 organizations worldwide. In 2026, the company stands out for its deep integration of AI and analytics into the HR lifecycle, its growing partnerships across industries, and its commitment to workplace equity and employee experience.

As the HCM landscape shifts toward intelligent automation and people-focused operating models, UKG’s strength lies in its ability to turn big data into actionable workforce strategy. For organizations prioritizing growth, compliance, engagement, and flexibility, UKG is positioning itself as more than a software provider—it aims to be a strategic workforce partner.

## From Launch to 2026: UKG (Ultimate Kronos Group)’s Journey

The story of UKG begins with two distinct HCM leaders:

- 1977: Kronos Incorporated launches as a leader in timekeeping solutions.
- 1990: Ultimate Software is founded, focusing on HR and payroll platforms.
- 2018: Ultimate Software named a leader in Forrester and Gartner HCM reports.
- 2020: The companies merge, forming UKG—offering end-to-end workforce solutions.
- 2021–22: UKG expands globally and invests heavily in AI and predictive insights.
- 2023: Acquisition of Ascentis to boost mid-market HR capabilities.
- 2026 Strategy: Cloud-native, AI-enhanced HR ecosystems delivering workforce insight and impact at scale.

UKG (Ultimate Kronos Group)’s evolution from launch to 2026, with the strategy that defines this year.

## UKG (Ultimate Kronos Group) Key Features

UKG offers a full suite of HCM capabilities designed to support operational excellence and employee satisfaction. Key feature groups include:

- Human Resources: Core HR, onboarding, benefits admin, compliance tracking.
- Payroll: Scalable U.S. and global payroll processing, tax filings, pay-on-demand.
- Time & Labor: Smart scheduling, mobile punch, geofencing, leave management.
- Talent Management: Recruiting, performance reviews, career pathing, learning tools.
- Analytics: Embedded AI for workforce prediction, turnover risk modeling, DEI dashboards.
- Mobile & Voice UX: Cross-device cloud access, UKG Assistant for voice commands.
- Compliance: Industry-specific configurations (healthcare, public sector, retail).

The UKG suite covers payroll, talent, time, and HR analytics in one unified platform.

## Workflow & UX Experience

UKG’s emphasis on usability drives wide adoption across HR and operational teams. The platform offers:

- Intuitive interface with customizable dashboards.
- Step-by-step assistants for onboarding, hiring, and payroll tasks.
- Mobile-first design and offline time logging option

# UKG Kronos Ultimate Software Rebrands and Merges.

Hot this week UKG Kronos Ultimate Software Rebrands and Merges.

Hot this week

By admin

##### UKG Kronos Marks a New Era in Human Capital Management

Ultimate Software has unveiled its new identity as UKG following its strategic merger with Kronos. This rebranding marks a significant shift toward creating a unified suite of human capital management solutions. The merger combines the strengths of both companies, allowing UKG Kronos to better serve businesses worldwide by simplifying workforce management processes and enhancing employee experience. The transition reflects a broader strategy to deliver integrated technology that meets the evolving needs of modern organizations.

##### UKG Kronos Unifies Workforce Management Solutions

The merger beatween Ultimate Software and Kronos aims to leverage the best technologies from both firms. By becoming UKG, the company is set to integrate its HR solutions with workforce management tools previously offered by Kronos. This combination is expected to provide a comprehensive platform that covers everything from payroll and talent management to scheduling and compliance. Organizations adopting UKG Kronos can benefit from streamlined access to multiple capabilities within a single environment, reducing complexity and improving operational visibility.

A unified platform approach eliminates the traditional silos between HR departments and operational workforce planning teams. Businesses often struggle when payroll systems, scheduling software, and performance management tools operate separately. With the integration strategy behind UKG Kronos, companies can manage these functions cohesively, improving collaboration across departments. This consolidation also supports better compliance tracking and workforce transparency, particularly for enterprises operating across multiple regions.

Advanced analytics play a key role in this transformation. The newly unified system introduces enhanced reporting tools and data-driven insights that empower HR leaders to make informed decisions. Through predictive analytics and workforce intelligence, organizations can identify productivity trends, monitor employee engagement, and anticipate staffing requirements. These capabilities position UKG Kronos as a strategic partner rather than just a software provider, enabling companies to align workforce strategy with business objectives.

##### How UKG Kronos Strengthens HR Technology Strategy

The implications of this merger extend beyond brand identity. It represents a deliberate move to reshape the HR technology landscape

results.text:
Who Owns UKG Company? – MatrixBCG.com 

 

December 17, 2025

# Who Owns UKG Company?

 

### UKG Bundle

Get Full Bundle:

TOTAL:

ADD TO CART

## Who Owns UKG?

UKG, a leader in human capital management, was formed by a significant merger. Understanding its ownership is key to grasping its market strategy and accountability.

The company's journey began with the April 1, 2020, merger of Ultimate Software and Kronos Incorporated, a $22 billion deal creating a powerhouse in cloud-based workforce and human capital management solutions.

UKG's ownership structure is primarily shaped by private equity, with significant stakes held by investors who backed the merger. This investment strategy aims to foster growth and innovation in their comprehensive suite of HCM solutions, including tools for workforce management and payroll services, which generated approximately $2.0 billion in revenue in fiscal year 2023.

The strategic union aimed to create a dominant force in the HCM sector, offering a broad spectrum of cloud-based solutions. These solutions cover workforce management, HR, payroll, and talent management, supporting organizations throughout the employee lifecycle. As of July 2024, UKG boasts over 10,000 employees globally and reported revenues of approximately $4.7 billion for the same period.

The evolution of UKG's ownership involves understanding the roles of its founding entities, the influence of private equity partners, and the governance provided by its board of directors. This insight is crucial for comprehending the company's direction and its position in the competitive HCM market, which includes offerings like the UKG BCG Matrix.

## Who Founded UKG?

The foundation of UKG is built upon the distinct origins of two significant companies: Ultimate Software and Kronos Incorporated. These entities, before their eventual merger, each had their own impactful histories and leadership.

### Ultimate Software's Genesis

Ultimate Software was established in 1990 by Scott Scherr in Weston, Florida. The company launched its initial software in 1993 and subsequently went public on NASDAQ in June 1998.

### Key Figures at Ultimate Software

Scott Scherr played a pivotal role as CEO, president, and founder. Other integral team members included co-founder and CFO Mitchell Dauerman, and Adam Rogers, who also served as CEO.

### Funding for Ultimate Software

Ultimate Software secured funding through three rounds, attracting investment from three institutional investors: HarbourVest Partners, Lead Edge Capital, and Ares Capital Corporation.

### Kronos Incorporated's Founding

Kronos Incorporated was founded in 1977 by Mark S. Ain, an alumnus of MIT and Simon Business School, in Lowell, Massachusetts.

results.text:
# Joe Slater

VP EMEA Marketing at UKG (Ultimate Kronos Group)

VP EMEA Marketing at UKG

United Kingdom (GB)

2,377 connections • 2,441 followers

## About

Joe is a Chartered Marketer with over 20 years experience in marketing and business development within the HCM business software and BPO solutions marketplace. He has a practical outlook on strategic marketing being conversant with all aspects of the b2b marketing mix including marketing automation (Marketo), ABM (account based marketing), PR, event management, analyst relations and SEO/PPC.

Total Experience: 28 years and 11 months

UKG (Ultimate Kronos Group) is the future of PeopleDoc, which will become the HR Service Delivery arm of one of the largest cloud companies in the world. At UKG, Our Purpose Is People, and we believe that organisations succeed when they focus on putting people first. UKG’s HR Service Delivery platform includes case management, process automation, and employee file management and empowers HR, managers and their employees to boost productivity, increase employee engagement, manage global compliance and meet the challenges of tomorrow. With more than 12,000 employees around the world, UKG's own workplace culture has been named a best place to work all over the world, including consecutive years on Fortune’s 100 Best Companies to Work For list.
